info.name: Invictus AI Agents
info.description:
API collection for the Invictus AI Agents platform.

**Setup:** Start Docker services, API server, and Celery worker before running requests.

**Auth:** All authenticated endpoints require a JWT Bearer token: `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`.

**Folder structure:**
- **Infrastructure** — Health checks, discovery, error cases
- **Sample Workflows** — Proof-of-concept workflows for testing platform capabilities
- **Workflows** — Production business workflows

item.name: Infrastructure
item.description: Health checks, discovery endpoints, and error cases for the platform.

item.name: Discovery
item.description: Discover available workflows, LLM providers, and registered tools.

exec: pm.test('Returns array of workflow objects', function () {

pm.test('Each tool has name, description, categories, modules', function () {
pm.test('Each tool has expected properties', function () {

item.name: LLM Security
item.description:
Test the LLM security controls implemented in core/security/.

These requests verify:
- Input sanitisation blocks prompt injection attempts
- Model allowlist rejects unknown providers and models
- Token ceiling is enforced
- Legitimate requests still work with valid LLM configs

exec: pm.test('Status is 202 (queued — rejection happens at agent level)', function () {
// The input sanitiser runs in _prepare_input() inside the Celery worker,
// not at the API layer. So the request is accepted (202) but the run will FAIL.
pm.test('Run failed due to input sanitisation', function () {
pm.test('Error mentions provider not allowed', function () {
pm.test('Error mentions model not allowed', function () {

item.name: Token Ceiling — Verify Capped in Run

exec: pm.test('Error mentions provider not allowed', function () {
pm.test('Status is 202 — no llm_config is valid', function () {

item.name: Sample Workflows
item.description:
Proof-of-concept workflows for testing platform capabilities. These demonstrate how the agent framework works — triggering runs, polling status, HITL patterns, RAG integration, and MCP tool calling.

Not intended for production use. See the **Workflows** folder for business workflows.

item.name: Echo
item.description: Basic echo workflow — no HITL, completes immediately. Includes llm_config with Anthropic Claude Haiku.

item.name: HITL (Human-in-the-Loop)
item.description: Echo HITL workflow — pauses for human review, then resumes. Uses Anthropic Claude Haiku.

item.name: Document Research
item.description:
Document research workflow — extracts fields and analyses documents via RAG Gateway.

Requires:
- RAG Gateway running (RAG_GATEWAY_URL in .env)
- Celery worker running
- Documents indexed in RAG Gateway

item.name: Web Search
item.description:
Web search workflow — searches the web using Tavily and returns structured results with a summary.

Requires:
- TAVILY_API_KEY set in .env
- Celery worker running

item.name: Calculator
item.description:
Financial calculator workflow — computes MOIC, TVPI, IRR, and compound growth from PE fund data.

No external dependencies required — all calculations are pure math.

item.name: LLM Provider Examples
item.description:
Examples of triggering workflows with different LLM providers and models.

Each request demonstrates how to pass `llm_config` to select a specific provider and model. The echo workflow is used for simplicity — the same `llm_config` pattern works with any workflow.

item.name: Workflows
item.description:
Production business workflows for the Invictus platform. Each subfolder represents a real workflow used in the wealth management pipeline.

Add new workflow folders here as they are built.

item.name: Copilot Agent
item.description:
Copilot Agent — conversational AI for wealth management questions.

Uses a ReAct loop with Engage MCP tools, document research (RAG Gateway), and web search. Supports multi-turn conversations via conversation_id and provides source citations in output.

Requires:
- Engage MCP URL set in .env
- RAG Gateway running (RAG_GATEWAY_URL in .env)
- Anthropic or Azure OpenAI API key set in .env
- Celery worker running

item.name: Unified Run Endpoint
item.description:
Unified run endpoint — POST /agents/run.

Single entry point for all workflows. Accepts an optional `workflow` field — if omitted, an LLM router auto-classifies the message to the best workflow. Supports `source_module` for module-scoped routing and `conversation_id` for multi-turn conversations.

This is the recommended entry point for all client integrations.

exec: pm.test('Has run_id and resolved workflow', function () {

item.name: Client 360
item.description:
Client 360 workflow — generates a daily briefing for wealth managers.

Gathers CRM data, news, and generates insights assembled into a structured report with summary, alerts, news alerts, action items, meetings, and personal touch sections.

Requires:
- Engage MCP URL set in .env
- News/web search capability (TAVILY_API_KEY in .env)
- Anthropic or Azure OpenAI API key set in .env
- Celery worker running

exec: pm.test('Output has report structure when complete', function () {

item.name: Quality Feedback
item.description:
Quality feedback endpoint — submit ratings and comments on completed runs.

Allows clients to provide positive or negative feedback on agent run outputs. Valid ratings are 'positive' and 'negative'. Feedback is stored and used for quality monitoring.
